response: Japan, a nation of approximately 125 million people, boasts the world’s third-largest economy with a GDP of $4.2 trillion, though its growth remains modest at 1.1%. Despite a low unemployment rate of 2.6%, the country faces economic challenges, including an inflation rate of 3.2% and an exceptionally high debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%. With a life expectancy of 84.6 years and exports totaling $756 billion, Japan remains a global leader in both economic influence and public health.
